---
title: "Merge feature tables"
subtitle: "Combine dense feature spaces into long or wide tables."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You have several `FeatureSeries` outputs sampled on compatible time axes and want a modeling table.

```{python}
import numpy as np

from natural_features.core.feature_types import FeatureSeries
from natural_features.core.interchange import merge_feature_tables
from natural_features.features.common import extractor_metadata

audio = FeatureSeries(
    values=np.array([[1.0], [2.0]], dtype=np.float32),
    times_s=np.array([0.0, 1.0]),
    coords={"feature": ["rms"]},
    metadata=extractor_metadata("demo.rms", params={}),
)
motion = FeatureSeries(
    values=np.array([[0.1], [0.3]], dtype=np.float32),
    times_s=np.array([0.0, 1.0]),
    coords={"feature": ["motion"]},
    metadata=extractor_metadata("demo.motion", params={}),
)

features = {
    "audio.rms": audio,
    "vision.motion": motion,
}
```

## Wide format

```{python}
merge_feature_tables(features, format="wide")
```

## Long format

```{python}
merge_feature_tables(features, format="long").loc[:, ["time_s", "feature", "value", "output_name"]]
```

## Boundary it closes

Wide output is intentionally restricted to dense `FeatureSeries` values. Event and track outputs need long/object tables because they do not share a single dense feature axis.

## See also

[Table interchange](../concepts/table-interchange.qmd)
